name: girder-worker

metadata.name: girder-worker
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Batch execution engine built on celery.

documentation:
---
substitutions:
  docs-status: |-
    ```{image} https://readthedocs.org/projects/girder-worker/badge?version=latest
    :alt: Documentation Status
    :target: https://girder-worker.readthedocs.io
    ```
  license-badge: |-
    ```{image} docs/license.png
    :alt: License
    :target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/girder_worker
    ```
  logo: |-
    ```{image} docs/static/favicon.png
    ```
---

# {{ logo }} Girder Worker {{ docs-status }} {{ license-badge }}

A flexible, simple script execution engine that integrates with the
[Girder](http://girder.readthedocs.org) data management system to run
distributed batch jobs.

The worker supports running tasks in many environments including Python, R,
and Docker, and supports fetching and pushing data to
and from various data sources, including Girder.

Visit the [documentation](http://girder-worker.readthedocs.org) for more details and API documentation.

We'd love for you to [contribute to Girder Worker](CONTRIBUTING.md).

functions.name: setup.prerelease_local_scheme
documentation:
Return local scheme version unless building on master in CircleCI.

This function returns the local scheme version number
(e.g. 0.0.0.dev<N>+g<HASH>) unless building on CircleCI for a
pre-release in which case it ignores the hash and produces a
PEP440 compliant pre-release version number (e.g. 0.0.0.dev<N>).

classes.name: setup.CustomInstall
documentation:
Override the default install to add some custom install-time behavior.
Namely, we create the local config file.

functions.name: girder_worker.app.gw_task_prerun
documentation:
Deserialize the jobInfoSpec passed in through the headers.

This provides the a JobManager class as an attribute of the
task before task execution.  decorated functions may bind to
their task and have access to the job_manager for logging and
updating their status in girder.

Pre-girder 3.0 plugins supported the same entrypoint (girder.plugin) to register
themselves.  If a Girder 2.x instance is installed with this package, it will
attempt to load this entrypoint and fail with an error message.  Due to how
pre-3.0 plugins worked, it still falls back to the built-in version of worker
and functions correctly.  The logic below eliminates that error message.
First ensure girder is installed, otherwise it doesn't make sense to import
this module at all.

functions.name: girder_worker.girder_plugin.event_handlers.validateSettings
documentation:
Handle plugin-specific system settings. Right now we don't do any
validation for the broker or backend URL settings, but we do reinitialize
the celery app object with the new values.

functions.name: girder_worker.girder_plugin.event_handlers.schedule
documentation:
This is bound to the "jobs.schedule" event, and will be triggered any time
a job is scheduled. This handler will process any job that has the
handler field set to "worker_handler".

functions.name: girder_worker.girder_plugin.event_handlers.cancel
documentation:
This is bound to the "jobs.cancel" event, and will be triggered any time
a job is canceled. This handler will process any job that has the
handler field set to "worker_handler".

Handle plugin-specific system settings. Right now we don't do any
validation for the broker or backend URL settings, but we do reinitialize

functions.name: girder_worker.girder_plugin.utils.getWorkerApiUrl
documentation:
Return the API base URL to which the worker should callback to
write output information back to the server. This is controlled
via a system setting, and the default is to use the core server
root setting.

functions.name: girder_worker.girder_plugin.utils.girderInputSpec
documentation:
Downstream plugins that are building Girder worker jobs that use Girder IO
should use this to generate the input specs more easily.

```
param resource

The resource document to be downloaded at runtime.

type resource

dict

param resourceType

The resource type to download for the input. Should
be "folder", "item", or "file".

type resourceType

str

param name

The name of the resource to download. If not passed, uses
the "name" field of the resource document.

type name

str or None

param token

The Girder token document or raw token string to use to
authenticate when downloading. Pass  for anonymous downloads.

type token

dict, str, or None

param dataType

The worker  field.

type dataType

str

param dataFormat

The worker  field.

type dataFormat

str

param fetchParent

Whether to fetch the whole parent resource of the
specified resource as a side effect.

type fetchParent

bool
```

functions.name: girder_worker.girder_plugin.utils.girderOutputSpec
documentation:
Downstream plugins that are building worker jobs that use Girder IO
should use this to generate the output specs more easily.

```
param parent

The parent to upload the data into (an item or folder).

type parent

dict

param token

The Girder token document or raw token string to use to
authenticate when uploading.

type token

dict or str

param parentType

The type of the parent object ("item" or "folder").

type parentType

str

param name

Name of the resource to use when uploading. Required if
the output target type is "memory". If the target is "filepath", uses
the basename of the file being uploaded by default.

type name

str or None

param dataType

The worker  field.

type dataType

str

param dataFormat

The worker  field.

type dataFormat

str

param reference

Optional "reference" string to pass back to the server
during the upload. This can be used to attach arbitrary data to this
for tracking purposes, e.g., referring back to related inputs. Bind to
the "data.process" event to hook into the upload and inspect references.

type reference

str
```

```
str or dict

param logPrint

Whether standard output from the job should be
```

Decorator that populates a girder_worker celery task with

---

back to Girder over HTTP (seconds).

methods.name: girder_worker.utils.JobManager._flush
documentation:
If there are contents in the buffer, send them up to the server. If the
buffer is empty, this is a no-op.

methods.name: girder_worker.utils.JobManager.write
documentation:
Append a message to the log for this job. If logPrint is enabled, this
will be called whenever stdout or stderr is printed to. Otherwise it
can be called manually and will still perform rate-limited flushing to
the server.

```
param message

The message to append to the job log.

type message

str

param forceFlush

Whether to force the write of this event to the
server. Useful if you don't expect another update for some time.

type forceFlush

bool
```

methods.name: girder_worker.utils.JobManager.updateStatus
documentation:
Update the status field of a job.

```
param status

The status to set on the job.

type status

JobStatus
```

methods.name: girder_worker.utils.JobManager.updateProgress
documentation:
Update the progress information about a job.

```
param total

The total progress value, or None to leave the same.

type total

int, float, or None

param current

The current progress value, or None to leave the same.

type current

int, float, or None

param message

Progress message to set, or None to leave the same.

type message

str or None

param forceFlush

Whether to force the write of this event to the
server. Useful if you don't expect another update for some time.

type forceFlush

bool
```

methods.name: girder_worker.utils.JobManager.refreshStatus
documentation: Refresh the status field from Girder

This class can be used to write log messages to Girder by capturing
stdout/stderr printed within the context and sending them in a
rate-limited manner to Girder. This is not threadsafe since it changes
the global values of sys.stdout/sys.stderr.

It also exposes utilities for updating other job fields such as progress
and status.

methods.name: girder_worker.docker.io.girder.GirderFileStreamReader.read
documentation:
Implementation note: due to a constraint of the requests library, the
buf_len that is used the first time this method is called will cause
all future requests to `read` to have the same `buf_len` even if
a different `buf_len` is passed in on subsequent requests.
Stream a file from Girder.

functions.name: girder_worker.docker.utils.select_loop
documentation:
Run a select loop for a set of readers and writers

```
param exit_condition

A function to evaluate to determine if the select
loop should terminate if all pipes are empty.

type exit_condition

function

param readers

The list of ReaderStreamConnector's that will be added to the
select call.

type readers

list

param writers

The list of WriteStreamConnector's that will be added to the
select call.

type writers

list
```

functions.name: girder_worker.docker.utils.chmod_writable
documentation:
Since files written by docker containers are owned by root, we can't
clean them up in the worker process since that typically doesn't run
as root. So, we run a lightweight container to make the temp dir cleanable.

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.stream_adapter.StreamPushAdapter

methods.name: girder_worker.docker.stream_adapter.StreamPushAdapter.write
documentation: Write a chunk of data to the output stream.

methods.name: girder_worker.docker.stream_adapter.StreamPushAdapter.close
documentation: Close the output stream. Called after the last data is sent.

This represents the interface that must be implemented by push adapters for
IO modes that want to implement streaming output.

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.stream_adapter.JobProgressAdapter
documentation:
This reads structured JSON documents one line at a time and sends
them as progress events via the JobManager.

```
param job_manager

The job manager to use to send the progress events.

type job_manager

girder_worker.utils.JobManager
```

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.stream_adapter.DockerStreamPushAdapter

methods.name: girder_worker.docker.stream_adapter.DockerStreamPushAdapter._read_header
documentation:
Read the header or part of the header. When the head has been read, the
payload size is decoded and returned, otherwise return None.

methods.name: girder_worker.docker.stream_adapter.DockerStreamPushAdapter._read_payload
documentation:
Read the payload or part of the payload. The data is written directly to
the wrapped adapter.

An adapter that reads a docker stream. The format is a Header and a Payload (frame)
where the header has the following format:

```none
header := [8]byte{STREAM_TYPE, 0, 0, 0, SIZE1, SIZE2, SIZE3, SIZE4}
```

We want to read the header to get the size of the payload, read the payload
and forward it on to another adapter.

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.io.FDStreamConnector

methods.name: girder_worker.docker.io.FDStreamConnector.open
documentation: Open the stream connector, delegated to implementation.

methods.name: girder_worker.docker.io.FDStreamConnector.close
documentation: Close the stream connector, delegated to implementation.

methods.name: girder_worker.docker.io.FDStreamConnector.fileno
documentation:
This method allows an instance of this class to used in the select call.

:returns: The file descriptor that should be used to determine if the connector
         has data to process.

FDStreamConnector is an abstract base class used to connect a read(input) and write(output)
stream.

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.io.FDWriteStreamConnector

methods.name: girder_worker.docker.io.FDWriteStreamConnector.fileno
documentation:
This method allows an instance of this class to used in the select call.

:returns: The file descriptor for write(output) side of the connection.

methods.name: girder_worker.docker.io.FDWriteStreamConnector.write
documentation:
Called when it is detected the output side of this connector is ready
to write. Reads (potentially blocks) at most n bytes and writes them to
the output ends of this connector. If no bytes could be read, the connector
is closed.

:param n The maximum number of bytes to write.
:type n int
:returns: The actual number of bytes written.

methods.name: girder_worker.docker.io.FDWriteStreamConnector.open
documentation: Calls open on the output side of this connector.

methods.name: girder_worker.docker.io.FDWriteStreamConnector.close
documentation: Closes the output side of this connector, followed by the input side.

FDWriteStreamConnector can be used to connect a read and write stream. The write
side of the connection will be used in the select loop to trigger write i.e
the file descriptor from the write stream will be used in the select call.

This is typically used to stream data to a named pipe that will be read inside
a container.

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.io.FDReadStreamConnector

methods.name: girder_worker.docker.io.FDReadStreamConnector.fileno
documentation:
This method allows an instance of this class to used in the select call.

:returns: The file descriptor for read(input) side of the connection.

methods.name: girder_worker.docker.io.FDReadStreamConnector.read
documentation:
Called when it is detected the input side of this connector is ready
to read. Reads at most n bytes and writes them to the output ends of
this connector. If no bytes could be read, the connector is closed.

:param n The maximum number of bytes to read.
:type n int
:returns: The actual number of bytes read.

methods.name: girder_worker.docker.io.FDReadStreamConnector.open
documentation: Calls open on the input side of this connector.

methods.name: girder_worker.docker.io.FDReadStreamConnector.close
documentation: Closes the output side of this connector, followed by the input side.

FDReadStreamConnector can be used to connect a read and write stream. The read
side of the connection will be used in the select loop to trigger write i.e
the file descriptor from the read stream will be used in the select call.

This is typically used to stream data from a named pipe that is being written
to inside a container.

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.io.StreamReader

methods.name: girder_worker.docker.io.StreamReader.read
documentation:
Stream readers must implement this method, which is responsible for
reading up to `buf_len` bytes from the stream. For now, this is
expected to be a blocking read, and should return an empty string to
indicate the end of the stream.

methods.name: girder_worker.docker.io.StreamReader.close
documentation: Close the input stream. Called after the last data is read.
This represents the interface that must be implemented by a stream reader.

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.io.StreamWriter

methods.name: girder_worker.docker.io.StreamWriter.write
documentation: Write a chunk of data to the output stream.

methods.name: girder_worker.docker.io.StreamWriter.close
documentation: Close the output stream. Called after the last data is sent.
This represents the interface that must be implemented by a stream writer.

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.io.FileDescriptorReader
documentation: Reader to read from a file descriptor.

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.io.FileDescriptorWriter
documentation: Writer to write to a file descriptor.

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.io.StdStreamWriter
documentation: Writer for write to stdout and stderr.

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.io.NamedPipe
documentation: A named pipe.

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.io.NamedPipeReader

methods.name: girder_worker.docker.io.NamedPipeReader.path
documentation:
The argument to pass to the docker container's entrypoint. In this case
the path to the named pipe.
Reader to read from a named pipe.

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.io.NamedPipeWriter

methods.name: girder_worker.docker.io.NamedPipeWriter.path
documentation:
The argument to pass to the docker container's entrypoint. In this case
the path to the named pipe.
Write to write to a named pipe.

methods.name: girder_worker.docker.io.ChunkedTransferEncodingStreamWriter.write
documentation:
Write a chunk of data to the output stream in accordance with the
chunked transfer encoding protocol.

methods.name: girder_worker.docker.io.ChunkedTransferEncodingStreamWriter.close
documentation: Close the output stream. Called after the last data is sent.

classes.name: girder_worker.GirderWorkerPluginABC

methods.name: girder_worker.GirderWorkerPluginABC.task_imports
documentation: Plugins should override this method if they have tasks.

Abstract base class for Girder Worker plugins. Plugins must descend from this
class; see the {ref}`plugins` section for more information.

classes.name: girder_worker.log_utils.StdOutHandler
documentation:
Because we re-bind sys.stdout/stderr dynamically to redirect
print statements to Girder job logs, we must also dynamically
refer to sys.stdout dynamically in our custom log handler.

functions.name: girder_worker.docker.tasks._pull_image
documentation: Pulls the specified Docker image onto this worker.

functions.name: girder_worker.docker.tasks.docker_run
documentation:
This task runs a docker container. For details on how to use this task, see the
{ref}`docker-run` guide.

```
param task

The bound task reference.

type task



param image

The docker image identifier.

type image

str

param pull_image

Whether to explicitly pull the image prior to running the container.

type pull_image

bool

param entrypoint

Alternative entrypoint to use when running the container.

type entrypoint

str

param container_args

Arguments to pass to the container.

type container_args

list

param volumes

Volumes to expose to the container.

type volumes

dict

param remove_container

Whether to delete the container after the task is done.

type remove_container

bool

return

Fulfilled result hooks.

rtype

list
```

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.tasks._SocketReader
documentation:
Used to mediate the difference between the python 2/3 implementation of docker-py
with python 2 attach_socket(...) returns a socket like object, with python 3
it returns an instance of SocketIO.

functions.name: girder_worker.docker.transforms.girder.setPermissions
documentation:
Set permissions on download assets so that workers can have full read
access to them regardless of user.  For directories, they have full general
access.

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.transforms.girder.ProgressPipe
documentation:
This can be used to stream progress information out of a running docker container as
part of a docker_run task. For a usage example, see {ref}`docker-progress`.

```
param name

The filename, which will be a named pipe open for reading from the host.

type name

str

param volume

The bind mount volume where the underlying named pipe will reside.

type volume

```

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.transforms.girder.GirderFileIdToStream
documentation:
This can be used to stream a Girder file into a docker container. See
{ref}`docker-run-streaming-input` for example usage.

```
param _id

The Girder file ID.

type _id

str or ObjectId
```

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.transforms.girder.GirderFileIdToVolume
documentation:
This can be used to pass a Girder file into a docker container. It downloads
the file to a bind mounted volume, and returns the container path of the file.

```
param _id

The Girder file ID.

type _id

str or ObjectId

param volume

The bind mount volume where the file will reside.

type volume



param filename

Alternate name for the file. Default is to use the name from Girder.

type filename

str
```

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.transforms.girder.GirderFolderIdToVolume
documentation:
This can be used to pass a Girder folder into a docker container. It downloads
the folder to a bind mounted volume, and returns the container path of the directory.

```
param _id

The Girder folder ID.

type _id

str or ObjectId

param volume

The bind mount volume where the directory will reside.

type volume



param folder_name

Alternate name for the directory. Default is to use the name from Girder.

type folder_name

str
```

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.transforms.girder.GirderItemIdToVolume
documentation:
This can be used to pass a Girder item into a docker container. It downloads
the item to a bind mounted volume, and returns the container path of the directory.

```
param _id

The Girder item ID.

type _id

str or ObjectId

param volume

The bind mount volume where the item will reside.

type volume



param item_name

Alternate name for the file. Default is to use the name from Girder.

type item_name

str
```

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.transforms.girder.GirderUploadVolumePathToItem

methods.name: girder_worker.docker.transforms.girder.GirderUploadVolumePathToItem.transform
documentation: We ignore the "result"

This transform uploads data in a bind mount volume to a Girder item. This should be used
in `girder_result_hooks` to upload files produced by the task.

```
param volumepath

The location of the file to upload.

type volumepath



param item_id

The item ID in Girder.

type item_id

str or ObjectId

param delete_file

Whether to delete the file afterward.

type delete_file

bool
```

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.transforms.girder.GirderUploadVolumePathToFolder
documentation:
This transform uploads data in a bind mount volume to a Girder folder. This should be used
in `girder_result_hooks` to upload data produced by the task.

```
param volumepath

The location of the file or directory to upload.

type volumepath



param folder_id

The folder ID in Girder.

type folder_id

str or ObjectId

param delete_file

Whether to delete the data afterward.

type delete_file

bool
```

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.transforms.girder.GirderUploadVolumePathJobArtifact
documentation:
This transform can be used to upload artifacts produced during a docker task execution
and attach them to the corresponding job in Girder. This can be useful for tracing and
debugging jobs, or simply collecting intermediate information during job execution. If
the passed in path does not exist, this is a no-op.

```
param volumepath

A volume path pointing to a mounted directory or file. If a directory,
all files within the directory will be uploaded as artifacts to the job. If a file,
just uploads the single file. If it does not exist, no action is performed.

type volumepath



param job_id

The job ID to attach the artifacts to. If calling this from Girder via
docker_run.delay, you will not need to set this, as it will be set automatically.

type job_id

str

param name

A name for the artifact. Only applies for single file paths. If not specified,
will use the basename of the file.

type name

str

param upload_on_exception

If True, this transform will occur even if the docker task
fails. This can be used to debug failed docker_run tasks.

type upload_on_exception

bool
```

classes.name: girder_worker.task.Task

methods.name: girder_worker.task.Task.canceled
documentation:
A property to indicate if a task has been canceled.

```
return

True is this task has been canceled, False otherwise.

rtype

bool
```

Girder Worker Task object. Tasks defined by plugins must be subclasses of this class,
however you will typically not need to reference it yourself, as it will be automatically
instantiated by the girder_worker celery app. See {ref}`creating-tasks` for instructions.

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.transforms.HostStdOut
documentation:
Represents the standard output stream on the host machine. Can be used with
{py:class}`girder_worker.docker.transforms.Connect` to write text to stdout.

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.transforms.HostStdErr
documentation:
Represents the standard error stream on the host machine. Can be used with
{py:class}`girder_worker.docker.transforms.Connect` to write text to stderr.

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.transforms.ContainerStdOut
documentation:
Represents the standard output stream of the container. Can be used with
{py:class}`girder_worker.docker.transforms.Connect` to redirect the containers
standard output to another stream.

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.transforms.ContainerStdErr
documentation:
Represents the standard error stream of the container. Can be used with
{py:class}`girder_worker.docker.transforms.Connect` to redirect the containers
standard error to another stream.

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.transforms.BindMountVolume
documentation:
A volume that will be bind mounted into a docker container.

```
param host_path

The path on the host machine.

type host_path

str

param container_path

The path in the container this volume will be mounted
at.

type container_path

str

param mode

The mounting mode

type mode

str
```

methods.name: girder_worker.docker.transforms._TemporaryVolumeMetaClass.default
documentation: This returns the default temporary volume that is always mounted into the container.

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.transforms.TemporaryVolume

methods.name: girder_worker.docker.transforms.TemporaryVolume.__init__
documentation: Note that this mode is explicitly set with os.chmod. What you
set, is what you get - no os.makedirs umask shenanigans.

This is a class used to represent a temporary directory on the host that will
be mounted into a docker container. girder_worker will automatically attach a default
temporary volume. This can be reference using `TemporaryVolume.default` class attribute.
A temporary volume can also be create in a particular host directory by providing the
`host_dir` param.

```
param host_dir

The root directory on the host to use when creating the
the temporary host path.

type host_dir

str

param mode

The default mode applied to the temporary volume if it does
not already exist.

type mode

int
```

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.transforms._DefaultTemporaryVolume
documentation:
Place holder who delegates implementation to instance provide by transform(...) method
An instance of the class is returned each time `TemporaryVolume.default` is accessed.
When the docker_run task is executed the transform(...) method is call with an instance
containing information about the actual default temporary volume associated with the
task. The place holder then delegates all functionality to this instance.

documentation: The path within the docker container.

methods.name: girder_worker.docker.transforms.NamedPipeBase.host_path

classes.name: girder_worker.docker.transforms.Connect

documentation: The method is called before save the argument in the job model.

This utility class represents the connection between a
{py:class}`girder_worker.docker.transforms.NamedOutputPipe` or
{py:class}`girder_worker.docker.transforms.NamedInputPipe` and one of the other streaming
transforms. Girder Worker will stream the data to or from the named pipe.

```
param input

The input side of the connection

type input

 or


param output

type output

 or
 or
```
